message:
[LOW] fcp-crypto: metamorphic HPKE cross-swap-of-ciphertexts-between-AADs test

Two sealed boxes encrypt the same plaintext under two different AADs
(`for_zone_key` with differing node_id). The test:
1. confirms each box opens under its own (enc, aad),
2. swaps ciphertexts while keeping the original `enc` headers,
3. asserts BOTH cross-opens fail.

Strictly stronger than the existing hpke_wrong_aad_fails, which uses one
fresh AAD that never corresponded to a legitimate seal. The cross-swap
leg catches a regression where the AAD encoding stops participating in
the AEAD tag — in that world the tampered test still fails (ciphertext
differs), wrong_aad still fails (AAD differs), but the cross-swap box
would pass because (enc_A, ct_B, aad_A) could be made consistent with
a buggy AEAD contract.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-crypto --lib hpke_cross_swap → 1 passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
